problems.hint: 과거의 사실과 반대되는 상황을 말할 때는 ‘had + p.p.’와 ‘would have + p.p.’를 함께 사용합니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 혼합가정법이 사용된 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: If I were you, I will go there.
If I had known her, I would meet her now.
If I had known her, I would be talking to her now.
If I knew her, I would met her yesterday.
If I knew her, I would have met her.

problems.explanation: 혼합가정법은 과거의 사실이 현재의 결과에 영향을 주는 가정으로, ‘If + had p.p., would + 동사원형’ 구조를 사용한다.
problems.hint: 과거의 일이 현재의 상황에 영향을 준다면 혼합가정법(If + had p.p., would + 동사원형)을 씁니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 중 직접화법을 간접화법으로 바꾼 문장이 올바른 것을 고르시오.
 
 He said, “I am tired.”

choices.inst: He said that he is tired.
He said that he was tired.
He said that I was tired.
He said he will be tired.
He said that he had been tired.

problems.explanation: 직접화법에서 간접화법으로 바꿀 때, 시제는 한 단계 과거로 이동하므로 ‘is → was’가 된다.
problems.hint: 간접화법에서는 일반적으로 시제를 한 단계 과거로 바꿉니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 중 명령문을 간접화법으로 바꾼 문장이 올바른 것을 고르시오.
 
 He said to me, “Close the door.”

choices.inst: He told me close the door.
He said me to close the door.
He told me to close the door.
He said that I close the door.
He told to me to close the door.

problems.explanation: 명령문을 간접화법으로 바꿀 때는 ‘tell + 목적어 + to부정사’ 구조를 사용한다.
problems.hint: 명령문은 간접화법에서 ‘tell + 사람 + to + 동사원형’으로 바뀝니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 병렬구조의 오류가 있는 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: She wants to travel and to study abroad.
He likes reading and to write letters.
I need to clean and to organize my room.

problems.explanation: ‘reading’은 동명사이고 ‘to write’는 to부정사로 형태가 일치하지 않아 병렬구조 오류가 발생한다.
problems.hint: 연결되는 두 요소는 반드시 같은 문법적 형태여야 합니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 비교급이 올바르게 사용된 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: She is more taller than her sister.
This book is interestinger than that one.
He runs faster than I do.
My bag is heavier that yours.
This movie is most exciting than the other.

problems.explanation: 비교급 문장은 ‘비교급 + than’ 구조로, ‘He runs faster than I do.’가 문법적으로 정확하다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 원급 비교가 올바르게 사용된 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: She is as tall than her brother.
She is so tall as her brother.
She is as tall as her brother.
She is the tall as her brother.
She is taller as her brother.

problems.explanation: 원급 비교는 ‘as + 형용사 + as’ 구조로, 3번 문장이 올바른 형태이다.
problems.hint: ‘as + 형용사 + as’는 두 대상을 ‘같은 정도로’ 비교할 때 사용합니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 최상급이 올바르게 사용된 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: This is the most best restaurant in town.
He is the most tall student in the class.
She is the tallest of the two girls.
Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the world.
Today is more coldest than yesterday.

problems.explanation: 최상급은 ‘the + 최상급 + in/of ~’ 구조로 사용되며, 4번 문장이 문법적으로 정확하다.
problems.hint: 최상급은 ‘the + 최상급 형용사/부사 + in(of) + 범위’의 형태를 가집니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 부정어가 문두에 올 때 도치가 올바르게 사용된 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: Never I have seen such a beautiful view.
Never have I seen such a beautiful view.
Never I seen have such a beautiful view.
Never did I saw such a beautiful view.
Never has I seen such a beautiful view.

problems.explanation: 부정어(Never)가 문두로 오면 ‘조동사 + 주어 + 동사’ 순으로 도치가 일어나므로 2번이 정답이다.
problems.hint: ‘Never, Rarely, Seldom’이 문두에 오면 조동사와 주어의 위치가 바뀝니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 조건절에서 도치가 사용된 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: If I were you, I would apologize.
Were I you, I would apologize.
If had I known it, I would have helped you.
Had I knew it, I would have helped you.
If I had knew it, I would have helped you.

problems.explanation: 가정법 조건절에서 ‘if’를 생략할 때, 주어와 동사의 위치를 바꿔 도치형으로 만든다. ‘Were I you’가 그 예다.
problems.hint: 조건절 도치는 ‘If + 주어 + 동사’ 대신 ‘동사 + 주어’ 형태로 바꾼 것입니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 강조구문(It ~ that)이 올바르게 사용된 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: It was in 2020 when I met her first.
It is she who helped me with the project.
It is she which helped me with the project.
It was at the park where we first meet.
It were my parents who gave me this gift.

problems.explanation: 강조구문은 ‘It + be동사 + 강조할 부분 + who/that ~’ 형태로, 사람일 경우 ‘who’를 사용한다.
problems.hint: 사람을 강조할 때는 ‘who’, 사물이나 장소를 강조할 때는 ‘that’을 사용합니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 장소를 강조하는 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: It was John that fixed the computer.
It was yesterday that we went shopping.
It was in Paris that we first met.
It is she who can solve the problem.
It was at night when he called me.

problems.explanation: ‘in Paris’는 장소를 나타내므로, ‘It was in Paris that ~’는 장소를 강조하는 강조구문이다.
problems.hint: 장소를 강조할 때는 ‘It was in + 장소 + that ~’ 형태를 사용합니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 강조구문이 아닌 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: It was my mother that cooked dinner.
It is this song that reminds me of home.
It was when I was ten that I learned to swim.
It is because I was tired that I went home early.
It was cold that I wore a coat.

problems.explanation: ‘It was cold that ~’은 원인(‘because’)의 의미가 없으므로 문법적으로 올바른 강조구문이 아니다.
problems.hint: 강조구문은 ‘It + be동사 + 강조 부분 + that ~’의 완전한 문장 구조를 가져야 합니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 가주어(it)가 올바르게 사용된 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: To finish this work will take a long time.
It to finish this work will take a long time.
It will take a long time to finish this work.
Finishing this work it will take a long time.
It takes a long time finishing this work.

problems.explanation: 가주어 ‘it’을 문두에 두고, 진주어 역할을 하는 ‘to부정사’를 문장 뒤에 두는 형태가 올바르다.
problems.hint: ‘It + be동사/동사 + 형용사/시간 + to부정사’ 구조를 떠올려 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 ‘that절’을 진주어로 쓰며 가주어를 사용한 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: That he passed the test surprised everyone.
It surprised everyone that he passed the test.
It that he passed the test surprised everyone.
It surprised that he passed the test everyone.
He passed the test it surprised everyone.

problems.explanation: 가주어 ‘it’이 문두에 오고, 진주어 ‘that절’이 문장 끝에 오는 ‘It + 동사 + that절’ 구조가 정답이다.
problems.hint: ‘It + 동사 + that절’은 ‘that절’ 전체가 주어 역할을 할 때 쓰이는 가주어 구문입니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 ‘It is ~ that’ 구조를 이용한 가주어 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: It is easy that solve this problem.
It is easy to solve this problem.
To solve this problem is easy it.
Solving this problem it is easy.
It easy to solve this problem is.

problems.explanation: 형용사 ‘easy’ 앞에 가주어 ‘it’을 두고, 진주어 역할의 ‘to부정사’를 뒤에 둔 구조가 옳다.
problems.hint: ‘It is + 형용사 + to부정사’는 일반적인 가주어 구문입니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 가목적어(it)를 올바르게 사용한 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: I found to learn English easy.
I found it easy to learn English.
I found easy to learn English it.
I found that to learn English easy.
I found it to learn English easy.

problems.explanation: ‘find it + 형용사 + to부정사’는 전형적인 가목적어 구문이다. 실제 목적어(to learn English)는 뒤에 위치한다.
problems.hint: ‘think, find, make, consider’ 뒤에 ‘it + 형용사 + to부정사’ 형태가 자주 옵니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 가목적어 구문으로 쓰인 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: She made it clear that she was not interested.
She made clear it that she was not interested.
She made that it clear she was not interested.
She made it to clear that she was not interested.
She made that she was not interested it clear.

problems.explanation: ‘make it + 형용사 + that절’ 구조는 가목적어 구문으로, 실제 목적어는 뒤의 ‘that절’이다.
problems.hint: 가목적어 구문은 ‘it’을 먼저 두고, 진목적어(that절·to부정사 등)을 뒤에 둡니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 가목적어 구문이 잘못 사용된 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: I think it important to be honest.
He found it difficult to solve the problem.
She considers it rude to interrupt others.
I made it to possible to finish the work early.
They believe it necessary to take action.

problems.explanation: ‘make it possible to ~’가 올바른 표현이므로, ‘to possible’은 잘못된 형태이다.
problems.hint: ‘make it possible to + 동사원형’ 형태를 기억하세요. ‘to possible’은 문법적으로 틀립니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 문장에서 밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 알맞은 단어를 쓰시오.
 
 If I ______ rich, I would travel around the world.
problems.explanation: 가정법 과거에서는 주어가 단수라도 ‘be동사’ 대신 ‘were’를 사용하는 것이 일반적이다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 문장에서 밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 알맞은 단어를 쓰시오.
 
 It is important ______ study hard for your future.
problems.explanation: ‘It is + 형용사 + to부정사’ 구문에서 진주어 역할을 하는 부정사 앞에는 ‘to’가 필요하다.
problems.hint: ‘It is + 형용사 + to부정사’ 형태를 떠올려 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 문장을 간접화법으로 바꾸시오.
 
 He said, “I will call you tomorrow.” → He said that he ______ call me the next day.
problems.explanation: 직접화법을 간접화법으로 바꿀 때 ‘will’은 한 시제 뒤로 밀려 ‘would’로 바뀐다.
problems.hint: 간접화법에서는 조동사의 시제가 한 단계 과거로 바뀝니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 안내문을 읽고 물음에 답하시오.
 
 [Community Fitness Center Notice]
 Due to the renovation of the main hall, all yoga classes will be held in Room 203 starting next Monday. Please check the updated class schedule on our website. For further inquiries, contact the reception desk.
 
 What should members do if they want to confirm the new class times?

choices.inst: Visit the center in person to ask the instructor.
Check the center’s website for the schedule.
Wait until the renovation is finished.
Attend the regular class time as usual.

problems.explanation: 안내문에 따르면 수업 일정은 웹사이트에서 확인하라고 안내되어 있다.
problems.hint: 안내문에서 'updated class schedule'과 관련된 부분을 찾아보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 표를 읽고 물음에 답하시오.
 
 [Bus Schedule: Downtown ↔ Airport]
 | Departure | Arrival | Duration | Note |
 |------------|----------|-----------|------|
 | 08:00 | 08:45 | 45 min | Express |
 | 08:30 | 09:30 | 60 min | Regular |
 | 09:00 | 09:45 | 45 min | Express |
 
 Which bus should a passenger take to arrive at the airport before 9 a.m.?
problems.explanation: 08:00에 출발하는 익스프레스 버스가 08:45에 도착하므로 9시 이전에 공항에 도착할 수 있다.
problems.hint: 도착 시간을 기준으로 9시 이전에 도착하는 버스를 찾아보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글을 읽고, 내용과 일치하지 않는 것을 고르시오.
 
 [Notice]
 The university library will extend its opening hours during the exam period. From June 10 to June 25, it will be open from 8 a.m. to midnight. Food is not allowed inside the reading rooms, but drinks with lids are permitted.

choices.inst: The library will be open longer during exams.
The library closes at midnight between June 10 and June 25.
Students can bring food into the reading rooms.
Drinks with lids are allowed inside.

problems.explanation: 지문에서 음식물 반입은 금지된다고 했으므로 3번이 틀린 내용이다.
problems.hint: ‘Food is not allowed’라는 부분을 다시 확인해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글을 읽고, 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.
 
 [Announcement]
 Our city’s annual marathon will be held this Sunday. The race will start at Central Park and finish at Riverside Stadium. Streets near the course will be closed from 6 a.m. to 1 p.m. Drivers are advised to use alternative routes.

choices.inst: The marathon will start at Riverside Stadium.
The marathon will take place on Saturday morning.
Drivers can use all streets as usual.
Streets near the marathon route will be closed temporarily.
The marathon will continue until late at night.

problems.explanation: 지문에 따르면 코스 주변 도로는 오전 6시부터 오후 1시까지 통제된다고 했다.
problems.hint: 도로 통제 시간에 대한 정보를 확인해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글을 읽고, 내용과 일치하지 않는 것을 고르시오.
 
 [Email]
 Subject: Volunteer Program Update
 Hello everyone,
 The next volunteer meeting will be held on Friday at 4 p.m. in Room B105 instead of the main hall. Please bring your ID cards for registration. We will discuss next month’s community cleanup event.

choices.inst: The meeting will be held in the main hall.
Volunteers should bring their ID cards.
The meeting time is at 4 p.m. on Friday.
They will talk about next month’s cleanup event.
The meeting location has been changed.

problems.explanation: 지문에서 회의 장소가 본관이 아닌 B105호로 변경되었다고 했으므로 1번이 틀린 문장이다.
problems.hint: ‘instead of the main hall’이라는 구절을 확인해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글의 주제로 가장 알맞은 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 Many people believe that success comes only from talent, but effort plays an equally important role. Even the most gifted individuals must practice regularly to improve their skills. Consistent effort helps people overcome weaknesses and reach their goals.

choices.inst: Success depends only on natural talent.
Effort is more important than luck.
Both talent and effort are necessary for success.
Only gifted people can achieve their dreams.

problems.explanation: 글의 중심 내용은 성공에는 재능과 노력 두 가지가 모두 필요하다는 것이다.
problems.hint: 글에서 ‘talent’와 ‘effort’가 어떤 관계로 설명되는지 주목해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글의 요지로 가장 알맞은 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 With the rise of online shopping, many people no longer visit local stores. However, supporting local businesses helps keep communities strong. Buying from nearby shops creates jobs and builds relationships among neighbors.

choices.inst: Online shopping is more convenient than visiting stores.
Local businesses benefit communities and deserve support.
Most people prefer buying products online.
Shopping locally costs more than buying online.
Communities no longer need small local stores.

problems.explanation: 글의 핵심은 지역 상점을 이용함으로써 지역 사회가 유지되고 강화된다는 것이다.
problems.hint: ‘supporting local businesses’가 글 전체에서 어떤 의미로 사용되었는지 생각해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 Listening to music while studying can help some students concentrate better, but for others, it may cause distractions. The effect of background music varies depending on personal preference and the type of task.

choices.inst: Music can always help people study efficiently.
The effects of background music on studying differ by person.
Students should never listen to music when studying.
Classical music improves memory and focus.
Listening to music is a waste of time while studying.

problems.explanation: 글은 배경 음악이 공부에 미치는 영향이 사람마다 다르다는 점을 중심으로 설명하고 있다.
problems.hint: ‘for some students’와 ‘for others’라는 표현에 주목해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글을 읽고, 밑줄 친 부분에 나타난 화자의 기분으로 가장 알맞은 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 After waiting for weeks, Lisa finally received an email from the art contest committee. She opened it with trembling hands and read, “Congratulations! Your painting has been selected for the national exhibition.” <u>Lisa couldn’t believe her eyes and jumped up with joy.</u>
problems.explanation: Lisa는 자신의 그림이 선정되었다는 소식을 듣고 기뻐하고 자랑스러워하는 감정을 표현하고 있다.
problems.hint: ‘jumped up with joy’라는 표현의 의미를 생각해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글의 밑줄 친 문장을 통해 알 수 있는 화자의 의도로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 Tom looked at his friend’s messy desk and said, <u>“Wow, it looks like a tornado just passed through here.”</u> His friend laughed and started cleaning up the papers.

choices.inst: To joke about the messy state of the desk
To warn his friend about a storm
To ask for help with his own desk

problems.explanation: Tom의 말은 실제 폭풍이 아니라, 친구의 책상이 어지럽다는 사실을 농담처럼 표현한 것이다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글의 전반적인 분위기로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 As the sun slowly set behind the mountains, the village became quiet. Children stopped playing, and only the sound of crickets filled the air. An old man sat on a bench, watching the fading light with a gentle smile.
problems.explanation: 글의 분위기는 해질 무렵의 고요하고 평화로운 마을 모습을 묘사하고 있다.
problems.hint: ‘quiet’, ‘gentle smile’, ‘fading light’ 등의 표현에서 어떤 감정이 느껴지는지 생각해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글을 읽고, 밑줄 친 부분이 일어난 이유로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 Mina was planning a picnic with her friends, but when she looked outside, dark clouds were gathering and thunder began to rumble. She sighed and sent a message: “Let’s postpone it to next weekend.”
 
 Why did Mina decide to postpone the picnic?

choices.inst: Her friends couldn’t come that day.

problems.explanation: 지문에 어두운 구름과 천둥이 등장하므로 날씨 악화가 소풍 연기의 원인임을 알 수 있다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글을 읽고, 밑줄 친 결과로 가장 알맞은 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 <u>Jason practiced the piano every day for months before the music contest.</u> On the day of the performance, he played confidently and received first place.

choices.inst: He decided to quit playing the piano.
He received an award in the contest.
He made many mistakes during his performance.
He was too nervous to perform.

problems.explanation: 지속적인 연습의 결과로 Jason이 대회에서 1위를 했다는 내용이 제시되어 있다.
problems.hint: ‘practiced every day’와 ‘received first place’의 관계를 파악해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글의 전개 방식으로 가장 알맞은 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 In the past, people relied on handwritten letters to communicate over long distances. Later, telephones made it possible to talk instantly. Today, smartphones allow people to send messages and video chat anytime, anywhere.

choices.inst: By describing events in chronological order
By listing reasons for an opinion
By presenting a problem and its solution

problems.explanation: 글은 과거에서 현재로 이어지는 의사소통 방식의 변화를 시간 순으로 제시하고 있다.
problems.hint: ‘In the past’, ‘Later’, ‘Today’와 같은 시간 표현에 주목해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글의 전개 방식을 가장 잘 설명한 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 Many people think working from home reduces productivity. However, research shows that remote workers often get more done because they can manage their time better and avoid long commutes.

choices.inst: By describing a sequence of actions

problems.explanation: 글은 재택근무에 대한 일반적인 생각과 연구 결과를 대조하여 전개하고 있다.
problems.hint: ‘However’ 이후의 내용이 앞부분과 어떤 관계를 가지는지 살펴보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글의 전개 방식으로 가장 알맞은 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 To keep plants healthy, water them regularly and place them where they can get enough sunlight. Also, remove dead leaves to prevent disease. Following these simple steps will help your plants grow well.

choices.inst: By suggesting steps in a process
By comparing different kinds of plants
By explaining reasons for a problem

problems.explanation: 글은 식물을 잘 키우기 위한 구체적인 절차와 순서를 안내하고 있다.
problems.hint: ‘First’, ‘Also’, ‘Following these steps’ 등의 절차적 표현에 주목해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.specific: 글에 포함된 표현의 함축적 의미를 추론하기
problems.problem:
다음 밑줄 친 부분의 의미로 가장 알맞은 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 When I told my teacher that I forgot my homework again, she said, <u>“You’re on fire with forgetting things these days.”</u>

choices.inst: The student is very passionate about studying.
The student keeps forgetting things frequently.
The teacher is proud of the student.
The teacher is giving a real compliment.

problems.explanation: ‘on fire’는 여기서 실제 불타는 것이 아니라 ‘계속해서 (잊어버리는 일을) 하고 있다’는 풍자적 표현이다.
problems.hint: ‘on fire’가 긍정적인 의미로 쓰이지 않았다는 점에 주목하세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.specific: 글에 포함된 표현의 함축적 의미를 추론하기
problems.problem:
다음 글의 밑줄 친 표현의 의미로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 After hearing the bad news, Kevin just sat there, staring at the floor. His friend said, <u>“Come on, Kevin. Don’t let it get you down.”</u>
problems.explanation: ‘Don’t let it get you down’은 ‘기운 내’ 또는 ‘낙담하지 마’라는 의미의 관용 표현이다.
problems.hint: ‘get you down’이 ‘기분을 떨어뜨리다’는 의미임을 떠올려 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.specific: 글에 포함된 표현의 함축적 의미를 추론하기
problems.problem:
다음 밑줄 친 문장의 의미로 가장 알맞은 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 When Sarah finally finished her long presentation, her classmate whispered, <u>“Well, that was a marathon.”</u>

choices.inst: The presentation was exhausting and long.

problems.explanation: ‘marathon’은 여기서 실제 경주가 아니라 길고 힘든 일을 비유적으로 표현한 것이다.
problems.hint: ‘marathon’이 단순히 운동이 아니라 ‘오랜 시간 지속된 일’을 의미할 수 있다는 점을 생각해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 중 ‘기후 변화의 영향’을 주제로 한 글의 첫 단락으로 가장 알맞은 것을 고르시오.
 
 Which of the following is the most appropriate opening paragraph for a factual essay about the effects of climate change?

choices.inst: Climate change is a global issue that causes rising temperatures, sea-level rise, and extreme weather events around the world.
I think summer is too hot these days, so I don’t like going outside.
Many people enjoy watching the sunset after a rainy day.
Climate change is scary! We must all stop it now.
The world has many beautiful seasons and different weather patterns.

problems.explanation: 1번 문장은 객관적 사실과 정보를 중심으로 주제를 제시하며, 사실적 정보 전달 글의 도입으로 적절하다.
problems.hint: 사실을 근거로 명확히 주제를 소개하는 문장을 찾아보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글의 내용으로 볼 때, 글의 목적을 가장 잘 나타낸 것은 무엇인가?
 
 [지문]
 This report describes how renewable energy sources, such as solar and wind power, can reduce air pollution. It also compares the energy efficiency of these sources with traditional fossil fuels.

choices.inst: To entertain readers with a story about energy
To persuade people to use only solar panels
To provide factual information about renewable energy
To describe the writer’s personal experience using energy
To criticize people who waste electricity

problems.explanation: 글은 재생에너지의 특성과 효율성을 객관적으로 비교·설명하고 있으므로 사실적 정보 전달이 목적이다.
problems.hint: 글의 어조가 감정적 설득보다는 객관적 설명 중심인지 확인해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 중 ‘인터넷의 역사’를 주제로 한 사실적 정보 전달 글의 전개 방식으로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
 
 Which of the following is the most appropriate way to organize a factual text about the history of the Internet?

choices.inst: By explaining the Internet’s development in chronological order
By sharing personal experiences using the Internet
By listing only the advantages of the Internet
By expressing opinions about social media
By describing a fictional story about computers

problems.explanation: 역사적 주제의 글은 일반적으로 시간 순서에 따라 사실을 나열하는 전개 방식이 적절하다.
problems.hint: ‘역사’라는 단어가 포함된 주제는 보통 시간의 흐름에 따라 설명됩니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 중 ‘자원봉사 경험’을 주제로 한 글의 첫 단락으로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
 
 Which of the following is the most appropriate opening paragraph for a writing about a volunteer experience?

choices.inst: Last summer, I volunteered at a local animal shelter, where I helped feed and care for abandoned dogs.
Many people like animals, and they are cute and friendly.
Animal shelters are sad places, and I don’t like visiting them.
I think volunteering is boring because it takes too much time.
There are many ways to help others, but I prefer staying home.

problems.explanation: 1번은 구체적인 시간, 장소, 활동이 포함되어 실제 경험을 기술하는 글의 도입으로 적절하다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글의 화자가 하고자 하는 계획으로 가장 알맞은 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 I’m planning to visit Busan next month with my friends. We will take the train in the morning and stay for two days. We want to try local seafood and visit Haeundae Beach.

choices.inst: To move to Busan for work
To travel to Busan with friends for a short trip
To write a report about Busan’s economy
To attend a conference in Busan

problems.explanation: 지문에서 ‘visit Busan next month with my friends’라고 했으므로 여행 계획을 말하고 있다.
problems.hint: ‘visit’, ‘stay for two days’ 등의 표현에 주목해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글의 화자가 말하고자 하는 경험으로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 Two years ago, I joined a school music club even though I couldn’t play any instruments. At first, I was nervous, but my friends helped me learn the guitar. After months of practice, we performed together at the school festival.

choices.inst: Learning how to play an instrument through a school club
Teaching classmates how to play guitar
Listening to a concert at school
Writing a song for a friend’s birthday

problems.explanation: 화자는 음악 동아리에서 친구들의 도움을 받아 기타를 배운 경험을 서술하고 있다.
problems.hint: ‘helped me learn the guitar’라는 구절이 핵심 단서입니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem: 다음 중 ‘독서의 중요성’에 대한 글로 가장 알맞은 의견 제시 문장을 고르시오.

choices.inst: Reading books helps people gain new knowledge and think critically.
Many people read books in libraries and bookstores.
Some books are longer than others.
Reading was more common in the past than it is now.

problems.explanation: 1번 문장은 읽기의 가치에 대한 필자의 생각을 드러내는 의견 표현이다.
problems.hint: 사실보다는 ‘왜 읽기가 중요한가’에 대한 평가적 진술을 찾아보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글을 읽고, 화자의 감정을 가장 잘 나타내는 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 When I finally reached the mountain top, I saw the sunrise spreading across the sky. I felt the cool wind on my face and realized how beautiful nature truly is.
problems.explanation: 자연의 아름다움을 깨닫고 평온함과 감탄을 느끼는 화자의 감정이 묘사되어 있다.
problems.hint: ‘beautiful nature’와 ‘sunrise spreading across the sky’의 어조를 살펴보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글의 필자가 전달하려는 주된 의견으로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 Using public transportation is better for the environment than driving a private car. It reduces air pollution and traffic congestion. If more people used buses or subways, cities would be cleaner and less crowded.

choices.inst: Public transportation should be used more often to protect the environment.
Cars are more comfortable than buses.
People should avoid traveling long distances.
Air pollution is not a serious problem in cities.
Subways are always crowded during rush hour.

problems.explanation: 글의 요지는 대중교통 이용이 환경 보호에 도움이 된다는 필자의 의견이다.
problems.hint: ‘better for the environment’라는 표현을 중심으로 글의 핵심 주장을 파악해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글을 읽고 중심 내용을 가장 잘 요약한 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 Many people throw away plastic bottles after using them once. However, recycling these bottles can save energy and reduce pollution. Everyone should try to reuse or recycle to protect the environment.

choices.inst: Recycling plastic bottles helps save energy and protect the environment.
Plastic bottles are light and easy to use.
Factories produce many plastic bottles every year.
It is better to use paper bottles instead of plastic ones.
People should drink more water to stay healthy.

problems.explanation: 글의 핵심은 플라스틱병을 재활용함으로써 환경을 보호하자는 내용이다.
problems.hint: 본문에서 반복적으로 강조된 'recycle'과 'protect the environment'를 중심으로 생각해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글을 읽고 중심 내용을 가장 잘 나타낸 문장을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 Exercise is not just good for your body; it also helps your mind. Regular physical activity can reduce stress and improve concentration. Even a short walk every day can make a difference.

choices.inst: Walking is the best exercise for everyone.
Exercise helps both physical and mental health.
Stress can be reduced by taking medicine.
Concentration is more important than fitness.
Most people dislike doing exercise regularly.

problems.explanation: 본문은 운동이 신체적 건강뿐 아니라 정신적 건강에도 긍정적인 영향을 준다고 요약할 수 있다.
problems.hint: ‘body’와 ‘mind’라는 두 가지 측면을 함께 언급한 문장을 찾아보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.problem:
다음 글의 내용을 가장 잘 요약한 것을 고르시오.
 
 [지문]
 Online learning allows students to study anytime and anywhere. It offers flexibility for those who have busy schedules. However, it also requires self-discipline and good time management skills.

choices.inst: Online learning gives freedom but requires self-control.
Students cannot focus well in online classes.
Online education is better than traditional learning.
Many students prefer learning in person.
The Internet makes studying harder for everyone.

problems.explanation: 글은 온라인 학습의 장점(자유로움)과 단점(자기 관리 필요)을 함께 제시하고 있다.
problems.hint: 글이 ‘장점과 함께 주의점’을 다루고 있다는 점에 주목해 보세요.

problems.subject: english
problems.specific: 목적과 대상에 맞게 내용과 형식을 갖춰 말하거나 글쓰기
problems.problem: 다음 중 ‘학교 축제 안내문’으로 가장 적절한 글을 고르시오.

choices.inst: Our school will hold its annual festival this Friday. There will be food booths, music performances, and games. Everyone is welcome to join!
I think school festivals are fun, but they can be too noisy.
I want to share my favorite memory from last year’s festival.
We should cancel the festival because it might rain.
School festivals are common in many countries.

problems.explanation: 1번은 축제의 일정과 내용, 참여를 권유하는 문구가 포함되어 안내문의 형식에 적합하다.
problems.hint: 안내문은 ‘언제, 어디서, 무엇을, 누가’ 등의 정보가 포함되어야 합니다.

problems.subject: english
problems.specific: 목적과 대상에 맞게 내용과 형식을 갖춰 말하거나 글쓰기
problems.problem: 다음 중 ‘교사에게 조언을 구하는 이메일’로 가장 알맞은 글을 고르시오.

choices.inst: Dear Mr. Kim, I’m having trouble managing my study time before exams. Could you please give me some advice? Thank you for your help. Best, Jina.
Hi! I’m really stressed these days. What should I do?
Mr. Kim, I think exams are too hard. Please make them easier.
Dear friend, I don’t want to study for the test anymore!
Hello teacher, I’m angry about the exam schedule!

problems.explanation: 1번은 정중한 인사말과 명확한 요청이 포함되어 교사에게 조언을 구하는 이메일 형식에 적절하다.
problems.hint: 공손한 어투와 명확한 요청 문장이 포함된 글을 찾아보세요.

problems.subject: english

problems.problem: 다음 중 ‘환경 보호 캠페인 포스터 문구’로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.

choices.inst: Save the Earth, Start with a Small Change!
